description_long: This object is an irregularly shaped, pale green fruit with a bumpy, dimpled surface and scattered dark spots resembling seeds. The surface texture is uneven, with pronounced nodules and indentations distributed across the entire exterior. The coloration transitions from a light yellow-green to a slightly darker green in some areas, with subtle brownish patches and small, dark speckles. There are no visible leafy structures or calyx at the top, and the object lacks the typical red color and conical shape of a common strawberry. The overall form is more oval and lumpy, with a matte finish and no visible shine. The object appears solid and dense, with no openings or interactive parts.
description: A pale green, bumpy fruit with scattered dark spots and an irregular, oval shape. The surface is matte and uneven, lacking any leafy structures or visible stem.
description_view_0: This view shows the side of the fruit with a mix of light and darker green hues, prominent dimples, and scattered dark spots.
description_view_1: The rear view reveals a more uniformly colored, slightly darker green surface with fewer visible spots and a rounded contour.
description_view_2: This side displays a lighter, almost whitish-green surface with pronounced bumps and a sparse distribution of dark spots.
description_view_3: The front view highlights the pale, matte surface with clustered nodules and a rounded apex, lacking any leafy or stem structures.
attribute_leaf_arrangement: No leaves are attached; the surface is bare with no visible leafy structures.
attribute_seed_pattern: Seeds are sparsely and irregularly distributed across the surface, embedded in the dimples with no clear pattern or clustering.
